plan_units.learning_objective: Explain how core traits of physicianship map directly to innovation leadership, supporting an identity shift from clinician to clinician-innovator.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as someone else’s identity to recognizing physicianship itself as innovation readiness through trait overlap and professional reframing
plan_units.rationale: This is the conceptual opener for the section and a foundational mindset reframe. It is best kept dense as a gestalt comparison because its value comes from seeing the overlap simultaneously rather than decomposing it.
source_refs: You do not need to learn an entirely new personality
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Map the clinical method to design thinking by identifying how history-taking, diagnosis, and treatment planning correspond to empathize, define, ideate, and prototype/test.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From perceiving design thinking as foreign jargon to recognizing it as a familiar clinical workflow through direct process mapping
plan_units.rationale: This is a high-value bridge concept, but the side-by-side flow itself is the teaching device. Keeping it dense preserves the simultaneous comparison clinicians need to internalize quickly.
source_refs: You run this innovation cycle twenty times a day
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify Sandra Lam’s framing of intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable formula grounded in balancing clinical vision with organizational realities.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as a vague personality trait to understanding it as a structured internal-change method through Lam’s formula and reading prompt
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly requires this reading block before Slide 3, and it introduces a named framework that informs the upcoming distinction between intrapreneurship and entrepreneurship.
source_refs: intrapreneurship is not just a personality trait
balance your clinical vision with the realities of your organizational environment
plan_units.title: Required Reading: Sandra Lam’s Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship in terms of where innovation happens and what constraints and resources define the work.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup creation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through contrast, constraint visualization, and organizational framing
plan_units.rationale: This is a core contrast concept with multiple explanatory beats and high pedagogical weight. It benefits from a cluster that starts with definitions, then visualizes institutional constraints, then resolves toward practical course purpose.
source_refs: improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship by recognizing the load-bearing institutional constraints that shape healthcare innovation.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup creation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through contrast, constraint visualization, and organizational framing
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial isolates the tension in the contrast: internal innovation must preserve existing institutional structures. It deepens learner understanding by converting abstract constraints into a memorable visual metaphor.
source_refs: without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur’s Maze: Why Internal Innovation Is Harder

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain the operational posture required of a medical intrapreneur: innovating within legacy systems, compliance rules, and budgets rather than bypassing them.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup creation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through contrast, constraint visualization, and organizational framing
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial lands the practical takeaway from the maze metaphor and aligns directly with the course’s focus on internal redesign and stakeholder-aware execution.
source_refs: legacy IT systems, compliance rules, and operational budgets
redesign the room without breaking the building
plan_units.title: Redesign the Room Without Breaking the Building

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish an idea from an opportunity by explaining that opportunities are rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating suggestions as innovation to engineering vetted organizational opportunities through definition, first-principles reframing, and worked problem formulation
plan_units.rationale: This is a central threshold concept for the lesson and deserves a full cluster. Learners must move from surface ideation to actionable opportunity framing, supported by definition, first-principles reframing, and worked example.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought or a suggestion
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain first-principles thinking as breaking a broken workflow down to fundamental truths before rebuilding a better system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating suggestions as innovation to engineering vetted organizational opportunities through definition, first-principles reframing, and worked problem formulation
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial introduces the complication and reframing needed to avoid superficial iteration. It is a perspective shift, making reframe the correct develop subtype within the cluster’s middle arc.
source_refs: Do not just iterate on "how we have always done it."
Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing to convert a superficial solution idea into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating suggestions as innovation to engineering vetted organizational opportunities through definition, first-principles reframing, and worked problem formulation
plan_units.rationale: This worked example provides concrete application, lowering abstraction and showing exactly how opportunity framing improves. It is the cluster’s exemplify step.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
Chief Complaint: Discharge times are delayed
The Opportunity: To design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: Worked Example: Bad Idea vs. Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Conclude the opportunity-framing sequence by reinforcing that the program’s goal is to move beyond idea generation toward executable, organization-specific opportunities.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating suggestions as innovation to engineering vetted organizational opportunities through definition, first-principles reframing, and worked problem formulation
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial closes the cluster by consolidating the operational standard learners are expected to adopt going forward.
source_refs: our goal is to stop generating ideas
plan_units.title: From Ideas to Actionable Opportunities

plan_units.learning_objective: Summarize the three major takeaways of Part 3 and prepare learners to transition from problem framing toward leadership identity in the next module.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From newly introduced innovation concepts to a consolidated clinician-intrapreneur mindset through recap and transition
plan_units.rationale: This is a synthesis slide whose value is the simultaneous recap across the section. It should remain a singleton because its function is consolidation and transition, not elaboration.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify recall and understanding of the section’s key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, clinical method vs. design thinking, Lam’s intrapreneurship formula, and core problem-framing principles.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to core concepts to verified comprehension through targeted formative retrieval
plan_units.rationale: The source includes an explicit knowledge check with five items. This belongs in scope as the assessment surface for the lesson and should remain a singleton assessment unit.
source_refs: what is essential for successfully scaling a solution
fall in love with the problem

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs primary-source grounding for Sandra Lam’s intrapreneurship framing so the workbook can cite the chapter-based basis for stakeholder alignment and organizational-fit claims.
Learner would benefit from supporting source basis for the entrepreneur versus intrapreneur distinction so the contrast reads as discipline-grounded rather than merely rhetorical.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded explanation of each overlapping trait—empathy, curiosity, analytical prowess, resiliency, and comfort with ambiguity—and how each transfers from patient care to operational innovation work.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The single-glance overlap showing physicianship and innovation leadership share core traits.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: You do not need to learn an entirely new personality
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain how one familiar clinical trait translates into innovation behavior in a hospital system.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the fuller identity-reframing argument that reduces resistance to the term innovator and helps clinicians see innovation as an extension of existing professional habits.
sections.title: Clinician Traits as Innovation Assets

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A more explicit step-by-step mapping between history, exam/labs, diagnosis, differential/treatment, and the design-thinking sequence, including why the analogy matters for workflow redesign.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The side-by-side process map and core bridge labels.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: When you take a patient's history, you are performing the first step of Design Thinking: **Empathize**.
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to translate a familiar clinical workflow step into its design-thinking equivalent.

sections.narrative_intent: Make the analogy sticky enough that learners can reuse it later when diagnosing a broken operational process rather than a patient problem.
sections.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking Translation

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Guided reading notes that unpack what makes intrapreneurship structured and replicable, with attention to internal stakeholder alignment and leveraging institutional assets.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The reading assignment and formula cue.

exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to summarize how Lam converts a vague concept into a repeatable internal-change method.

sections.narrative_intent: Support reading compliance while ensuring the text is processed for course-relevant implications rather than treated as standalone enrichment.
sections.title: Reading Companion: Sandra Lam’s Intrapreneurship Formula

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded comparison table of entrepreneur vs. intrapreneur conditions, including internal politics, budget limits, compliance, legacy systems, and scale advantages.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The high-level split between entrepreneurship and intrapreneurship.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners sort example barriers into intrapreneurial constraints versus blank-canvas startup conditions.

sections.narrative_intent: Build a more durable mental model of why internal innovation is not smaller entrepreneurship but a distinct operating discipline.
sections.title: Inside the Hospital: Why Intrapreneurship Demands Different Skills

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Longer-form explanation of feasibility, viability, and desirability in a hospital context, plus how first-principles thinking and root-cause analysis turn complaints into action pathways.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The core definition contrast between idea and opportunity.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to test a sample innovation suggestion against feasibility, viability, and desirability criteria.
Ask learners to convert a superficial complaint into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.

sections.narrative_intent: Carry the analytical depth that would overburden the slides, giving learners the disciplined framing language needed for later portfolio work.
sections.title: Engineering Opportunities, Not Just Ideas

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Short explanations for why each correct answer is right and why distractors fail, so the knowledge check doubles as retrieval plus clarification.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The assessment prompts and answer selection task.

exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to explain why one distractor is wrong for each key concept distinction.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the quiz from pure checking into a short remediation layer that reinforces distinctions likely to be confused.
sections.title: Review and Self-Check

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: Part 3 reframes clinicians as ready-made innovators, bridges clinical reasoning to design thinking, distinguishes intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship, and trains learners to move from weak ideas to vetted opportunities through first-principles and root-cause framing. The plan uses clusters only where progressive disclosure materially improves understanding: the intrapreneurship contrast and the idea-to-opportunity threshold concept. Synthesis, direct comparison, and assessment surfaces remain intentionally dense singletons.

Chief Complaint: Discharge times are delayed
The Opportunity: To design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: Worked Example: Bad Idea vs. Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Conclude the opportunity-framing sequence by reinforcing that the program’s goal is to move beyond idea generation toward executable, organization-specific opportunities.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating suggestions as innovation to engineering vetted organizational opportunities through definition, first-principles reframing, and worked problem formulation
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial closes the cluster by consolidating the operational standard learners are expected to adopt going forward.
source_refs: our goal is to stop generating ideas
plan_units.title: From Ideas to Actionable Opportunities

plan_units.learning_objective: Summarize the three major takeaways of Part 3 and prepare learners to transition from problem framing toward leadership identity in the next module.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From newly introduced innovation concepts to a consolidated clinician-intrapreneur mindset through recap and transition
plan_units.rationale: This is a synthesis slide whose value is the simultaneous recap across the section. It should remain a singleton because its function is consolidation and transition, not elaboration.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify recall and understanding of the section’s key distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, clinical method vs. design thinking, Lam’s intrapreneurship formula, and core problem-framing principles.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to core concepts to verified comprehension through targeted formative retrieval
plan_units.rationale: The source includes an explicit knowledge check with five items. This belongs in scope as the assessment surface for the lesson and should remain a singleton assessment unit.
